spec_match.rationale: Exercises a real surface of the spec's headline behaviour — <geolocation> element — declarative permission element that emits location directly…

interactive.rationale: Has 2 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles real failure modes — feature detection, permission rejection, fallback paths are all wired into the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5125006551416832 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: Mostly on the design system but 23 raw hex literal(s) leaked into inline CSS — should be replaced with the --accent / --text / --bg variables.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 2 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex colours with design-system variables
openQuestions.detail: Found 23 raw hex literal(s) in the inline <style> block. The shared system defines --accent-blue, --accent-emerald, --accent-rose, --bg-ivory, --bg-paper, --bg-stone, --text-black, --text-charcoal, --text-muted. Swap them in and re-check WCAG AA contrast.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: map-embed under the-geolocation-element: 3 pass / 3 partial / 0 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design-system drift, spec story complete.
